Patch Set 2:

> Any idea how to test if this patch is applied or not?

All I can think of is to 'grep' the code for __user.
 grep -A1 proc_dointvec .../include/linux/sysctl | grep '__user'

or something like that.
This is part of why I provided local versions of the required functions rather than have any expectations on what the kernel provides.

parents.subject: LU-13783 libcfs: support __vmalloc with only 2 args.

commit.subject: LU-13783 libcfs: don't depend on sysctl support for debugfs
commit.message:
LU-13783 libcfs: don't depend on sysctl support for debugfs

Since Linux v5.8-rc1~55^2~6 sysctl support routines like
proc_dointvec() expect a pointer to kernel-space, not userspace.

So stop using these function for debugfs files, and instead
provide bespoke functions.
